book_claims.mechanism: Compensates for common dietary deficiencies without providing toxically high doses of any single micronutrient
book_claims.text: Taking a multivitamin containing vitamin D, E, magnesium, A, calcium, potassium, and K every 2–3 days recommended as nutritional insurance

supplementary_research.finding: Physicians' Health Study II: daily multivitamin modestly reduced total cancer incidence by 8% in men over 11 years; no cardiovascular benefit observed
Multivitamin use associated with longer telomere length in older adults; effect size small but consistent with longevity-insurance rationale

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Take with food; fat-soluble; every-2-3-day dosing reduces toxicity risk
Commonly deficient; glycinate or malate forms best absorbed
Critical for those on plant-heavy diets; methylcobalamin preferred form

synergies.note: Book recommends both taken every 2–3 days as the core supplement stack for the Longevity Diet
Multivitamin is the recommended vehicle for vitamin D to avoid standalone high-dose toxicity risk
Multivitamin covers nutrients (B12, D) that plant-heavy diets may be low in
